docs.title: HeliosCTA Data Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the HeliosCTA backend data documentation. This guide is written for front-office users who need to understand what data is available, where it comes from, and how fresh it is.

docs.title: How to Use These Docs
docs.text:
• New here? Start with the Executive Summary for a one-page overview.
  • Looking for a specific dataset? Browse by domain below.
  • Unfamiliar with a term? Check the Glossary.
  • Need to know who owns what? See Owners & SLAs.

docs.title: Domain Index
docs.text: Domain Description Overview Power Electricity market data -- load, LMPs, fuel mix, outages, forecasts, tie flows across 7 ISOs Largest domain Meteologica Third-party power demand, generation, and price forecasts from Meteologica xTraders API 374 scrape scripts WSI (Weather Services International) Weather forecasts, degree days, temperature observations from WSI Trader API Temperature & degree-day focused EIA (Energy Information Administration) Government energy data -- generation mix, natural gas storage Public API source Genscape Natural gas production forecasts and daily pipeline production Proprietary data Positions & Trades Trading positions (Marex, NAV) and trade records (Clear Street, Marex) via SFTP Internal operations

docs.title: Architecture at a Glance
docs.text:
External APIs / SFTP
        |
   Python Scrapes  (backend/src/<domain>/)
        |
   Raw Tables      (Azure PostgreSQL)
        |
   dbt Models      (backend/dbt/)
        |
   Cleaned Views   (pjm_cleaned, meteologica_cleaned, etc.)
        |
   Front Office Dashboards / Analysis

Each scrape follows a standard pattern: _pull() -> _format() -> _upsert() -> main().

Pipeline health is tracked via PipelineRunLogger in the pipeline_runs table.

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text: HeliosCTA's backend ingests energy market data from multiple sources, stores it in Azure PostgreSQL, and transforms it via dbt into clean, analysis-ready views.

docs.title: What We Collect
docs.text: Category What Sources Approx. Tables Power Markets Load (actual, forecast, DA bids), LMPs (DA, RT), fuel mix, outages, tie flows, solar/wind forecasts PJM API, GridStatus, GridStatusIO ~70+ raw tables across 7 ISOs Power Forecasts Demand, generation, and DA price forecasts at ISO, region, and hub level Meteologica xTraders API ~374 raw tables Weather Temperature forecasts, degree-day forecasts (HDD/CDD), hourly observations WSI Trader API ~15 raw tables Natural Gas Production forecasts, pipeline production, underground storage Genscape API, EIA API ~4 raw tables EIA Hourly generation by fuel type, Form 860 generator attributes EIA Open Data API ~3 raw tables Positions & Trades Daily positions (Marex, NAV funds), trade records (Clear Street, Marex) SFTP file drops ~8 raw tables

docs.title: How Data Flows
docs.text:
1. Scrape scripts pull from external APIs or SFTP on a scheduled cadence (mostly via Prefect).
  2. Raw data lands in schema-separated tables in Azure PostgreSQL (e.g., pjm.da_hrl_lmps, eia.fuel_type_hrl_gen_test).
  3. dbt views clean, rename, join, and reshape raw data into analysis-ready views (e.g., pjm_cleaned.pjm_lmps_hourly).
  4. Event-driven pipelines (currently PJM DA LMPs) react to database notifications for near-real-time processing.

docs.title: Business Terms
docs.text: Term Definition ISO Independent System Operator -- the entity that manages the electric grid in a region. Major U.S. ISOs: PJM, ERCOT, MISO, CAISO, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP. LMP Locational Marginal Price -- the cost of electricity at a specific point on the grid, in $/MWh. DA (Day-Ahead) The market where electricity is bought and sold for delivery the next day. Prices are set by ~1 PM the day before. RT (Real-Time) The market where electricity is bought and sold for immediate delivery. Prices are set every 5 minutes. Load Electricity demand (consumption), measured in MW or MWh. Fuel Mix The breakdown of electricity generation by fuel source (gas, coal, nuclear, wind, solar, etc.). HDD / CDD Heating Degree Days / Cooling Degree Days -- measures of how much heating or cooling is needed relative to a base temperature (typically 65 deg F). Higher HDD = colder weather = more heating demand. WDD Weighted Degree Days -- degree days weighted by population or load to better reflect energy demand impact. Outage A period when a power plant is not generating, either planned (maintenance) or forced (breakdown). Measured in MW offline. Tie Flow Electricity flowing between grid regions via interconnection points (ties). Hub A trading point where electricity prices are set (e.g., Western Hub, Eastern Hub in PJM). Pricing Node A specific location on the grid where LMPs are calculated. RTO Regional Transmission Organization -- often used interchangeably with ISO. PJM is technically an RTO. Respondent In EIA data, the entity reporting generation data (e.g., PJM, ERCO for ERCOT, CISO for CAISO). BCF Billion Cubic Feet -- a unit for measuring natural gas volumes. SFTP Secure File Transfer Protocol -- used to exchange files with counterparties (brokers, prime brokers). NAV Net Asset Value -- here refers to the NAV fund administrator who provides position reports. Marex A futures broker that provides position and trade data. Clear Street A prime broker that provides trade confirmation data.

docs.title: Data Terms
docs.text: Term Definition Scrape An automated script that pulls data from an external source (API, SFTP) and loads it into our database. Upsert Insert new rows or update existing rows if they already exist (based on primary key). Prevents duplicates. dbt "data build tool" -- transforms raw data into clean, analysis-ready views using SQL. Mart A dbt model at the final layer, ready for consumption by dashboards and analysts. Staging A dbt model that cleans and renames raw source data but doesn't join tables. Source In dbt, a reference to a raw table loaded by a scrape. Ephemeral A dbt model that doesn't create a table/view; it's inlined as a CTE into downstream models. View A virtual table defined by a SQL query. Refreshes every time you query it. Primary Key The column(s) that uniquely identify each row. Used by upsert to detect duplicates. Grain What one row represents (e.g., one row per hour per region). Pipeline Run Logger Internal tracking system that records when each scrape ran, whether it succeeded, and how many rows were processed. Prefect The workflow orchestration tool that schedules and monitors scrape pipelines. T+1 Data available one business day after the event (e.g., end-of-day positions available next morning). Model Run In weather/forecast context, a specific execution of a forecast model (e.g., GFS 00Z run). GFS Global Forecast System -- a U.S. government weather model. ECMWF European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts -- a leading global weather model. AIFS Artificial Intelligence/Integrated Forecasting System -- ECMWF's AI-based weather model. Ensemble (ENS) A set of slightly varied forecast runs used to gauge forecast uncertainty. Operational (OP) The primary, highest-resolution run of a weather model.

docs.title: Monitoring
docs.text:
• All pipelines log to pipeline_runs table via PipelineRunLogger
  • Fields tracked: pipeline name, source, target table, start/end time, status (success/failure), rows processed, error details
  • Query pipeline_runs to check recent run status for any pipeline

docs.title: Failure Escalation
docs.text:
Severity Criteria Action P1 - Critical Real-time or DA data missing for >2 hours during market hours Immediate investigation P2 - High Forecast data (Meteologica, WSI) missing for >12 hours Investigate within 4 hours P3 - Medium Settlement/historical data delayed Investigate next business day P4 - Low Reference data or non-time-critical feeds Address within 1 week

Source for ownership data: Team leads should update this file as roles are assigned. Check pipeline_runs table for current pipeline health.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The EIA domain pulls publicly available U.S. energy data from the EIA Open Data API. Currently covers hourly generation by fuel type and weekly natural gas underground storage.

docs.title: Data Source
docs.text:
• API: EIA Open Data API v2 (https://api.eia.gov/v2/)
  • Authentication: API key (EIA_API_KEY)
  • Documentation: EIA Open Data Browser

docs.title: Scrape Inventory
docs.text: Script Table Description Source Endpoint fuel_type_hrl_gen_test eia.fuel_type_hrl_gen_test Hourly electricity generation by fuel type and respondent /electricity/rto/fuel-type-data weekly_underground_storage_test eia.weekly_underground_storage_test Weekly natural gas underground storage by region /natural-gas/stor/wkly eia_860_test eia.eia_860_test Generator attributes (capacity, fuel type, status) -- mirrors fuel_type format /electricity/rto/fuel-type-data

docs.title: Business Purpose
docs.text: Track real-time generation mix across the entire U.S. grid (US48 and all major ISOs/balancing authorities). Understand how much power is coming from gas, coal, nuclear, wind, solar, etc.

docs.title: Data Captured
docs.text:
• Grain: One row per hour per respondent (balancing authority)
  • Respondents covered: US48, PJM, ERCO, MISO, ISNE, NYIS, CISO, SPP, TVA, SOCO, and ~40+ other balancing authorities
  • Fuel columns: natural_gas, coal, nuclear, wind, solar, hydro, battery_storage, petroleum, geothermal, pumped_storage, other, and more
  • Primary key: datetime_utc, date, hour, respondent

docs.title: Refresh Cadence
docs.text:
• Trigger: Scheduled (Prefect)
  • Default pull window: Last 3 days (configurable)
  • Freshness: ~1-hour lag from EIA

docs.title: Business Purpose
docs.text: Track weekly natural gas in underground storage by region. This is a closely watched market indicator released every Thursday.

docs.title: Data Captured
docs.text:
• Grain: One row per week per region
  • Key fields: eia_week_ending, region, value (in BCF), series_description
  • Regions: East, Midwest, Mountain, Pacific, South Central, and sub-regions

docs.title: Refresh Cadence
docs.text:
• Trigger: Scheduled (Prefect)
  • Frequency: Weekly
  • Freshness: T+0 (same day as EIA release)

docs.title: Known Caveats
docs.text:
• Scripts have _test suffix -- these are production scripts despite the naming convention
  • EIA API uses pagination (5,000 row limit per request); scripts handle this automatically
  • No dbt cleaning views exist for EIA data -- consumed raw

docs.text: ~50+ balancing authorities organized by region: - National: US48 - Northeast: ISNE (ISO-NE), NYIS (NYISO) - Mid-Atlantic: PJM - Midwest: MISO, AECI, LGEE - Southeast: TVA, SOCO, SE, SEPA, DUK, CPLE, CPLW, SC, SCEG - Florida: FPL, FPC, FMPP, JEA, GVL, HST - Texas: ERCO (ERCOT) - Central: SWPP (SPP) - Northwest: BPAT, AVA, IPCO, NWMT, PACE, PACW, PGE, PSEI, SCL, etc. - Southwest: AZPS, DEAA, EPE - California: CISO (CAISO), BANC, LDWP, IID, TIDC

docs.title: Business Purpose
docs.text: Tracks weekly natural gas in underground storage by region. This is one of the most closely watched energy reports -- released every Thursday at 10:30 AM ET by the EIA. Storage levels relative to the 5-year average are a key driver of natural gas prices.

docs.title: Data Captured
docs.text: Field Description eia_week_ending The Friday that ends the reporting week region Storage region (East, Midwest, Mountain, Pacific, South Central, etc.) value Volume in BCF (Billion Cubic Feet) series_description Full EIA series description

docs.title: Primary Key
docs.text: eia_week_ending, region (derived)

docs.title: Known Caveats
docs.text:

  • Region is extracted from series_description via regex
  • Weekly frequency -- not suitable for intraday analysis

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Meteologica provides third-party power market forecasts via the xTraders API. This is the largest domain by script count (374 scripts), covering demand forecasts, generation forecasts (solar, wind, hydro), and day-ahead price forecasts for every major U.S. ISO and the L48 (Lower 48 states) aggregate.

docs.title: Business Purpose
docs.text: Meteologica forecasts give traders an independent, model-driven view of future power demand, renewable generation, and day-ahead prices. Comparing Meteologica forecasts against ISO-issued forecasts and actuals helps identify trading opportunities.

docs.title: Data Source
docs.text:
• API: Meteologica xTraders REST API
  • Authentication: JWT token auth, two accounts:
  • L48 account (helios_cta_us48): 35 scripts for US48 aggregate forecasts
  • ISO account (helios_cta): ~339 scripts for ISO-level and sub-regional forecasts
  • Auth module: backend/src/meteologica/auth.py
  • Rate limits: ~3 minute cooldown after burst of ~34 requests

docs.title: Script Organization
docs.text: Subfolder ISO Script Count Coverage l48/ US48 (aggregate) 35 National demand, generation, price forecasts pjm/ PJM 75 RTO + 3 regions + ~35 utility-level forecasts ercot/ ERCOT 49 RTO + zones + regional generation miso/ MISO 45 RTO + zones + regional generation caiso/ CAISO 40 RTO + sub-regions + renewables nyiso/ NYISO 41 RTO + zones + generation isone/ ISO-NE 39 RTO + zones + generation spp/ SPP 43 RTO + zones + generation

docs.title: Data Types
docs.text: Data Type Description Example Table Demand Forecast Hourly electricity demand forecast by region meteologica.usa_pjm_power_demand_forecast_hourly Generation Forecast Hourly solar, wind, or hydro generation forecast meteologica.usa_pjm_pv_power_generation_forecast_hourly DA Price Forecast Hourly day-ahead price forecast by hub/node meteologica.usa_pjm_da_power_price_system_forecast_hourly Observation Actual observed values for comparison meteologica.usa_caiso_power_demand_observation Long-Term Forecast Multi-week/month demand outlook meteologica.usa_caiso_power_demand_long_term_hourly Normal Historical average (normal) for comparison meteologica.usa_caiso_pv_power_generation_normal_hourly Projection Extended outlook based on blended models meteologica.usa_caiso_power_demand_projection_hourly

docs.title: Key Fields (Common Across Scripts)
docs.text:
• forecast_period_start / forecast_period_end -- the time window the forecast covers
  • model_run -- when the forecast was generated (helps track forecast vintages)
  • value -- the forecast value (MW for demand/generation, $/MWh for prices)
  • content_id -- Meteologica's internal ID for the data series

docs.title: Refresh Cadence
docs.text:
• Trigger: Scheduled (Prefect)
  • Frequency: Multiple times daily (new model runs)
  • Freshness: Latest model run typically within 6 hours

docs.title: dbt Views
docs.text:
Three cleaned mart views in meteologica_cleaned schema. See dbt views detail.

View Description meteologica_pjm_demand_forecast_hourly Cleaned PJM demand forecasts across all regions meteologica_pjm_generation_forecast_hourly Cleaned PJM generation forecasts (solar, wind, hydro) meteologica_pjm_da_price_forecast_hourly Cleaned PJM DA price forecasts across all hubs

docs.title: Known Caveats
docs.text:
• API rate limits require ~3 min cooldown after bursts; scripts are sequenced accordingly
  • Model run columns are stored as VARCHAR strings because azure_postgresql_utils does df.fillna(0) on insert
  • DST fall-back handling: normals/projections need forecast_period_end in the primary key to avoid duplicate-key conflicts
  • Only PJM forecasts currently have dbt cleaning views; other ISOs are raw-only

docs.text: Model Description staging_v1_meteologica_pjm_demand_forecast_hourly Unions and cleans all PJM demand forecast source tables staging_v1_meteologica_pjm_gen_forecast_hourly Unions and cleans all PJM generation forecast source tables staging_v1_meteologica_pjm_da_price_forecast_hourly Unions and cleans all PJM DA price forecast source tables

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Only PJM forecasts are cleaned via dbt; other ISOs (ERCOT, MISO, CAISO, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP) remain as raw tables
  • Model run values stored as VARCHAR (not timestamp) -- see Meteologica domain overview for why
  • Each staging model unions many source tables, so query performance depends on underlying table sizes

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: With 374 scripts, Meteologica is far too large to document individually. Scripts follow a consistent pattern within each ISO subfolder, so this page documents the patterns and provides representative examples.

docs.title: Standard Script Pattern
docs.text: Every Meteologica script: 1. Authenticates via backend/src/meteologica/auth.py (JWT token) 2. Pulls forecast data from the xTraders API for a specific content ID 3. Formats the response into a standardized DataFrame 4. Upserts to a table in the meteologica schema

docs.title: Naming Convention
docs.text:
usa_{iso}_{region}_{type}_power_{category}_forecast_hourly.py
  • {iso} = pjm, ercot, miso, caiso, nyiso, isone, spp, us48
  • {region} = optional sub-region (e.g., midatlantic, west_aep)
  • {type} = optional fuel type (e.g., pv, wind, hydro)
  • {category} = demand, generation, price

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This domain handles trading operations data -- position reports from brokers/administrators and trade confirmations from prime brokers. Data arrives via SFTP file drops and is loaded into the database, then cleaned via dbt into analysis-ready views.

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
SFTP File Drops (Marex, NAV, Clear Street)
        |
   Pull Scripts  (backend/src/postions_and_trades/tasks/pull_from_sftp/)
        |
   Raw Tables    (marex.*, nav.*, clear_street.* schemas)
        |
   dbt Models    (positions_cleaned, trades_cleaned schemas)
        |
   Email Scripts (send cleaned files to counterparties like MUFG, NAV)

docs.text:
• Positions: T+1 (files arrive overnight, pulled next morning)

  • Trigger: Scheduled (Prefect)

docs.title: Positions Cleaned (positions_cleaned schema)
docs.text:
The positions pipeline combines Marex and NAV position data through a multi-step staging process: 1. Combined -- Union Marex + NAV positions into one dataset 2. Forward Fill -- Fill missing values for positions held across dates 3. Add Columns -- Add derived fields (product codes, exchange info) 4. Exchange Codes -- Standardize exchange code formatting

Final mart: combined Marex + NAV positions with standardized fields.

docs.title: Trades Cleaned (trades_cleaned schema)
docs.text:
View Description clear_street_trades Cleaned end-of-day Clear Street trade confirmations clear_street_intraday_trades Cleaned intraday Clear Street trade confirmations marex_allocated_trades Cleaned Marex allocated (cleared) trades

Each trade staging pipeline adds product codes and standardized columns.

docs.title: Known Caveats
docs.text:
• Directory is misspelled as postions_and_trades (missing 'i') -- this is the actual folder name
  • SFTP file arrival times depend on counterparty systems and may be delayed
  • Trade break detection (nav_trade_breaks) is critical for operations -- any breaks need same-day resolution
  • Position forward-fill logic assumes missing dates mean the position is unchanged

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Power domain covers electricity market data across seven U.S. ISOs (Independent System Operators). It is the largest and most critical data domain, providing the load, pricing, generation, outage, and forecast data that drives daily trading and analysis.

docs.title: PJM Cleaned dbt Views
docs.text:
All views are materialized in the pjm_cleaned schema. The dbt pipeline follows a layered architecture:

Source (raw tables) -> Staging (clean/rename) -> Marts (join/aggregate, final views)

Source and staging models are ephemeral (not materialized as tables/views). Only mart models are exposed as views.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
GridStatus provides electricity market data across all 7 major U.S. ISOs. We ingest data via two channels:

  1. Open Source (gridstatus Python library) -- free, community-maintained
  2. Paid API (gridstatusio) -- commercial API with higher reliability

Both channels write to the gridstatus database schema.

docs.title: Paid API Scripts
docs.text: The paid API (gridstatusio_api_key/) covers the same ISOs with similar scripts. Key differences: - Uses API key authentication (GRIDSTATUS_API_KEY) - Higher reliability and data availability - Some additional fields (e.g., ercot_standardized_hourly.py, ercot_net_load_forecast.py)

docs.title: Refresh Cadence
docs.text:
• Trigger: Scheduled (Prefect via flows.py per ISO)
  • Frequency: Varies by data type (5-min for RT LMPs, hourly for fuel mix, daily for forecasts)
  • Freshness: Typically same-day with minutes-to-hours lag depending on data type

docs.title: Known Caveats
docs.text:
• Open source and paid API may have slightly different field names or data availability
  • PJM data exists in both PJM direct API and GridStatus -- GridStatus versions serve as backup/cross-validation
  • Each ISO subfolder has its own run.py and flows.py for orchestration

docs.title: Scrape Card
docs.text: Field Value Script backend/src/power/pjm/da_hrl_lmps.py Source PJM Data Miner 2 API Target Table pjm.da_hrl_lmps Schema pjm Trigger Scheduled + Event-Driven Cadence Daily (scheduled); also triggered via PostgreSQL NOTIFY (event-driven) Freshness Available by ~1:30 PM ET day-ahead Owner TBD

docs.title: Business Purpose
docs.text: Captures the day-ahead electricity prices at every pricing node in PJM. These prices are set the day before delivery and are the primary reference for power trading, hedging, and P&L calculations.

docs.title: Data Captured
docs.text: Field Description datetime_beginning_utc Hour start in UTC datetime_beginning_ept Hour start in Eastern Prevailing Time pnode_id Pricing node identifier pnode_name Human-readable node name voltage Voltage level equipment Equipment type type Node type (e.g., ZONE, HUB, AGGREGATE) total_lmp_da Total day-ahead LMP ($/MWh) energy_lmp_da Energy component of DA LMP congestion_lmp_da Congestion component of DA LMP marginal_loss_lmp_da Marginal loss component of DA LMP

docs.title: Primary Key
docs.text: datetime_beginning_utc, pnode_id

docs.title: Event-Driven Path
docs.text: In addition to scheduled pulls, this data also has an event-driven pipeline: - backend/src/power/event_driven/pjm/da_hrl_lmps.py listens for notifications_pjm_da_hrl_lmps on PostgreSQL - Managed by the listener service (backend/src/listeners/) - Retries up to 3 times with 5-second backoff on failure

docs.title: Downstream
docs.text:
• dbt view: pjm_cleaned.pjm_lmps_hourly (combined with RT LMPs)
  • dbt view: pjm_cleaned.pjm_lmps_daily (daily averages)

docs.title: Known Caveats
docs.text:
• PJM occasionally reissues DA prices (rebinding); later pulls will overwrite via upsert
  • Multiple node types exist -- filter by type to get zones, hubs, or individual generators

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: WSI provides weather-related data critical for energy trading -- temperature forecasts, weighted degree days (HDD/CDD), and historical temperature observations. Weather is the primary driver of electricity demand, making this domain essential for load forecasting and position management.

docs.title: Data Source
docs.text:
• API: WSI Trader (AG2) API
  • Authentication: Username/password (WSI_TRADER_USERNAME, WSI_TRADER_NAME, WSI_TRADER_PASSWORD)
  • Shared utilities: backend/src/wsi/utils.py (auth, HTTP client, CSV pull helpers)
  • City ID reference: backend/src/wsi/wsi_trader_city_ids.json

docs.title: Domain Subfolders
docs.text: Folder API Endpoint Description Scripts homepage_forecast_table/ GetCityTableForecast City-level min/max temp, avg temp, HDD/CDD forecasts 3 hourly_forecast/ GetHourlyForecast Hourly temperature forecasts by city 1 weighted_forecast_iso/ GetModelForecast Population-weighted temperature forecasts at ISO level 2 weighted_degree_day/ GetWeightedDegreeDayForecast Weighted degree day forecasts from multiple weather models 6 weighted_forecast_city/ GetWsiForecastForDDModelCities Population-weighted temperature forecasts at city level 1 historical_observations/ GetHistoricalObservations Actual observed hourly temperatures and daily WDD 2 natural_gas/ TBD Natural gas related forecasts (daily BCF) 1 reference/ N/A City ID lookup table (not run on schedule) 1

docs.title: Refresh Cadence
docs.text:
• Trigger: Scheduled (Prefect via flows.py in each subfolder)
  • WDD forecasts: Event-driven primary with scheduled reconciliation (see weighted_degree_day/)
  • Frequency: 2--4 times daily (aligned with weather model run times)
  • Freshness: Latest model run typically within 6--12 hours

docs.title: Key Concepts
docs.text:
• Multiple weather models: The WDD subfolder pulls from 6 different model sources (WSI blend, GFS op, GFS ens, ECMWF op, ECMWF ens, AIFS ens) to give traders a range of degree-day outlooks
  • City-level vs ISO-level: Some data is at individual city granularity; other data is population-weighted to ISO/region level
  • Observations vs Forecasts: Historical observations serve as the "truth" baseline for evaluating forecast accuracy

docs.title: Known Caveats
docs.text:
• No dbt cleaning views exist for WSI data yet -- all tables are consumed raw
  • DST transitions can cause duplicate key issues for some forecast types
  • City IDs must be kept in sync with WSI's reference data
